evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer.deriveNounCqlConstraint and CollocateProfileComparator.deriveAdjectivePattern both use a double-fallback Optional stream chain (.orElseGet(() -> grammarConfig.getRelations().stream()...orElse(FALLBACK))) — the nested stream-within-orElseGet is hard to parse and the two methods are structurally duplicated.

SketchHandlers.handleSketchRequest uses position-based URL routing: path.substring('/api/sketch/'.length()).split('/') — parts[0] is lemma, parts[1] might be 'dep' or a relation name. Adding a new path segment would require updating position-based array indices throughout.

ConlluConverter.convertConlluToWplChunks mixes two distinct responsibilities: (1) CoNLL-U sentence parsing state machine (tracking inSentence, emitting <s>/</s> tags), and (2) chunk file rotation (tracking sentencesInChunk, opening new files). These could be separated into a SentenceParser and a ChunkFileWriter.

SketchHandlers.executeRelationQueries uses BiFunction<RelationConfig, ExecutedSketch, Map<String, Object>> callback to vary response construction. The pattern avoids code duplication between the surface-sketch and dep-sketch paths, but makes it harder to trace the full data flow without following the lambda to its definition.
CollocateQueryHelper cleanly separates frequency lookup, collocate search, and scoring/ranking into distinct methods — good single-responsibility decomposition within the class.
ExplorationHandlers.validateExploreRequest extracts the shared preamble for three exploration handlers — correct extraction, reduces duplication.
GrammarConfigLoader decomposition: parse → parseAndValidateVersion → validateNoLegacyKeys → parseRelations → parseRelation. Each method has one clear responsibility. Well-structured.
BcqlHandlers: parseBcqlRequest (parse and validate) / handleBcqlQueryPost (execute) / buildBcqlResponse (format) — three-part handler decomposition is clean and testable.
SemanticFieldExplorer is a thin public facade delegating to CollocateProfileComparator and MultiSeedExplorer — the facade pattern is well-executed with clear delegation boundaries.
WordSketchApiServer.registerRoutes uses inline lambdas for all handler registration — simple handlers are appropriate inline; all complex handlers correctly delegate to named handler classes.

strengths: SemanticFieldExplorer facade pattern: the class delegates single-seed to itself (phased private methods), multi-seed to MultiSeedExplorer, and comparison to CollocateProfileComparator — clear ownership boundaries.
CollocateQueryHelper isolates all collocate search/score/rank concerns from BlackLabQueryExecutor — the extract was clean and complete.
GrammarConfigLoader is coherently decomposed into single-responsibility private methods that chain linearly: no method does more than one conceptual step.
BcqlHandlers three-part decomposition (parse/execute/format) is a model of how handlers should be structured for testability.
design_coherence.issue_character: The main design issues are: two structurally-parallel double-fallback stream chains that should be extracted to a shared utility; a large converter method that mixes parsing and file-rotation state machines; and position-based URL routing that is fragile to path structure changes.
design_coherence.score_rationale: The codebase has several well-designed components (facade pattern, handler decomposition, config loader) that show coherent design thinking. The score is in the mid-60s because three recurring patterns work against the overall coherence: the duplicated double-fallback stream expressions in SemanticFieldExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator signal a missing extraction; the position-based URL routing in SketchHandlers is fragile; and ConlluConverter mixes two separate responsibilities. These are not architectural failures but are design smells that accumulate maintenance cost.

issues.summary: deriveNounCqlConstraint and deriveAdjectivePattern duplicate a complex double-fallback stream pattern that should be extracted
evidence: CollocateProfileComparator.deriveAdjectivePattern: structurally identical double-fallback stream with different type filters
Both methods have the same structure: (1) filter by specific relation type AND POS group, (2) fall back to any matching POS group, (3) fall back to hardcoded constant
issues.suggestion: Extract a shared GrammarConfig utility method: RelationConfig.findBestRelationForPosGroup(GrammarConfig, PosGroup, RelationType...) that encapsulates the priority-ordered search. Replace both deriveNounCqlConstraint and deriveAdjectivePattern with calls to this utility.

issues.summary: SketchHandlers uses position-based array indexing for URL routing — fragile to path structure changes
evidence: String specificDeprel = parts.length > 2 ? parts[2] : null; — position 2 is the deprel
Adding /api/sketch/{lemma}/surface/{relation} would require updating position checks throughout
issues.suggestion: Replace array-position routing with named segment extraction (a simple utility method that maps URL pattern /api/sketch/{lemma}/{sub1}/{sub2} to named fields). Even a simple record SketchPath(String lemma, String sub1, String sub2) would make the intent explicit and changes local.

issues.summary: ConlluConverter.convertConlluToWplChunks mixes CoNLL-U sentence parsing state machine with chunk file rotation logic
evidence: Single method manages: inSentence boolean (sentence boundary tracking), sentencesInChunk counter (rotation trigger), writer management (open/close/rotate), and the complex finally block for counting
The method is ~60 lines with a 3-level nested try-finally and two distinct state machines interleaved
issues.suggestion: Extract sentence-boundary parsing into a private method processLine(line, state) that emits tokens/events, and keep chunk rotation logic in the main loop. This separation would make the finally-block counting logic easier to verify and make both concerns independently testable.

root_causes: The duplicate double-fallback streams in SemanticFieldExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator likely arose because both classes were written independently without sharing a GrammarConfig query utility.
Position-based URL routing is a consequence of using com.sun.net.httpserver which has no built-in path-parameter extraction.
